label: AWS RDS SQL Server
description: Connect to a Microsoft SQL Server instance managed by Amazon RDS using an endpoint, port, and credentials.

form.title: Connect AWS RDS SQL Server
form.subtitle: Enter your RDS endpoint, port, database name, and credentials to establish a connection.

methods.label: RDS Endpoint + Credentials
methods.description: Connect directly using your RDS endpoint, port, database name, username, and password. Works with SQL Server Authentication or any master user credentials created at instance launch.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via RDS Endpoint + Credentials

Find your RDS endpoint in the AWS Console and paste it along with your database credentials below.

### Steps

1. Open the [AWS RDS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/rds/) and navigate to **Databases**.
2. Click your SQL Server instance to open its detail page.
3. Under **Connectivity & security**, copy the **Endpoint** (e.g. `myinstance.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com`).
4. Note the **Port** — SQL Server defaults to `1433`.
5. Paste the endpoint into **Host** and the port into **Port** below.
6. Enter the **Database** name you want Anton to connect to (the master database is `master` by default).
7. Enter the **Username** and **Password** for a SQL Server login. The master user is created when you launch the instance.
8. Enable **Use SSL/TLS** if your parameter group or instance configuration enforces encrypted connections (recommended for production).

### Gotchas

- The RDS instance's **Security Group** must allow inbound TCP on port 1433 from the machine running Anton. Add an inbound rule if needed: **EC2 → Security Groups → Inbound rules → Add rule → MSSQL / TCP / 1433**.
- **Publicly accessible** must be set to `Yes` on the instance if Anton runs outside your VPC. Check under **Connectivity & security → Public accessibility**.
- IAM database authentication for SQL Server on RDS is not supported — use a native SQL Server login.
- SSL certificates for RDS are issued by Amazon. If certificate verification fails, download the [RDS CA bundle](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/UsingWithRDS.SSL.html) and configure your client accordingly.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host (RDS Endpoint)
fields.description: Found in the AWS RDS Console under Connectivity & security → Endpoint.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: SQL Server default is 1433. Change only if your instance uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Name of the SQL Server database Anton should connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: SQL Server login username. Typically the master user created at instance launch.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the SQL Server login above.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Use SSL/TLS
fields.description: Encrypt the connection using SSL/TLS. Strongly recommended for production RDS instances.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste a full ADO.NET or JDBC-style SQL Server connection string. Useful if you already have one from another tool or want to set advanced options like application name or timeout.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Connection String

Paste a full SQL Server connection string if you prefer to configure options directly.

### ADO.NET format example

`Server=myinstance.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com,1433;Database=mydb;User Id=admin;Password=secret;Encrypt=True;`

### Steps

1. Open the [AWS RDS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/rds/) → **Databases** → your instance → **Connectivity & security**.
2. Copy the **Endpoint** and **Port**.
3. Construct your connection string using the endpoint, port, database, username, and password.
4. Add `Encrypt=True;TrustServerCertificate=False;` for encrypted connections.
5. Paste the full string into the **Connection String** field below.

### Common parameters

- `Server=<host>,<port>` — host and port together
- `Database=<name>` — target database
- `User Id=<user>;Password=<pass>` — SQL Server credentials
- `Encrypt=True` — enable TLS
- `Connection Timeout=30` — seconds before connection attempt fails

### Gotchas

- Do not include credentials in a connection string that may be logged or version-controlled. Anton stores it encrypted at rest, but exercise caution.
- RDS SQL Server does not support Windows/Kerberos authentication from outside a domain-joined environment.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full ADO.NET-style SQL Server connection string including host, port, database, and credentials.
